original_abstract: Azabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes (aza-BCHs) and bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes (BCPs) represent a crucial class of *sp*³-rich motifs, frequently employed as three-dimensional bioisosteres to replace flat aromatic rings and enhance metabolic stability in drug candidates. Despite their utility, methods to directly interconvert or "scaffold hop" between these frameworks via single-atom skeletal editing remain scarce. Here, we report a strategy to bridge these two chemical spaces through a formal nitrogen-deletion process. By coupling photochemical [2+2] cycloadditions—which provide rapid access to multifunctionalized aza-BCHs—with a subsequent deamination step, we demonstrate the synthesis of bridge-functionalized BCPs. This approach addresses a significant synthetic gap, as BCPs with complex bridge substitution are typically difficult to access. This modular sequence offers a versatile pathway to diverse, privileged bicyclic scaffolds of high pharmaceutical relevance.
humanized_abstract: We identified azabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes (aza-BCHs) and bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes (BCPs) as a rich set of *sp*³-rich motifs, commonly used as three dimensional bioisoster for aromatic systems to impart greater metabolic stability to lead compounds. Methods to “scaffold hop” and directly interconvert these cores through single atom skeletal editing are scarce. Herein we establish a formal nitrogen deletion route connecting the two cores. We utilized photochemical [2+2] cycloadditions to generate multifunctionalized aza-BCHs, which were subsequently treated with a deamination catalyst to give bridge functionalized BCPs. BCPs with complex substitution patterns are typically hard to synthesize, making this method highly versatile for the generation of privileged bicyclic cores relevant to the pharmaceutical industry.

Methods to “scaffold hop” and directly interconvert these cores through single atom skeletal editing are scarce. Herein we establish a formal nitrogen deletion route connecting the two cores. We utilized photochemical [2+2] cycloadditions to generate multifunctionalized aza-BCHs, which were subsequently treated with a deamination catalyst to give bridge functionalized BCPs. BCPs with complex substitution patterns are typically hard to synthesize, making this method highly versatile for the generation of privileged bicyclic cores relevant to the pharmaceutical industry.
